behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: A mocked embedding function would hide real failures where the actual embedding model outputs vectors of unexpected dimensionality. The real failure occurs when the embedding service (API-based or local) produces vectors that don't match the vector database's index schema. Testing requires invoking the actual embedding service to verify correct dimensionality. Most production systems use API-based embeddings (OpenAI, Hugging Face API, etc.), making this a System-level concern, though local models would reduce it to Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute multiple test queries through the actual embedding service (real API endpoint or local instance). Verify returned vectors have dimensionality matching the vector database schema (e.g., 1536 for OpenAI, 384 for smaller models). Cross-reference against documented dimension for the embedding model. Test with diverse queries (short, long, special characters) to rule out edge cases in the embedding service.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: A mock vector database would hide failures in the real database's similarity ranking algorithm. The behavior depends on the vector database's actual implementation and correct application of cosine similarity. Testing requires a real vector database instance (local Weaviate, Qdrant, pgvector, or equivalent) whose behavior matches production to verify actual ranking order.
behaviors.test_description: Insert known test vectors with pre-computed cosine similarities into a real vector database instance. Query with test embeddings and verify returned results are ordered by descending similarity score. Test with multiple query vectors of varying similarity distributions (clustered, dispersed, identical-content duplicates). Validate ranking order against independently computed similarity scores.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends fundamentally on what a real LLM produces given nuanced semantic intent. A mocked LLM would not test whether the actual model understands complex, context-dependent queries and reorders appropriately. A mock would always return a predetermined order, hiding real failures where the LLM fails to recognize nuanced intent. Real LLM inference is required to verify reasoning capability.
behaviors.test_description: Create test queries with deliberately nuanced intent: context-dependent queries, implicit user needs, multi-faceted requirements, or queries requiring trade-off reasoning. Call the real LLM re-ranking step with initial similarity-ranked results. Verify the re-ranked order differs from input order and that reordering aligns with the nuanced intent. Test with queries where naive similarity ranking would miss the user's true intent.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: A mock database could easily return an empty set, but the real failure is when the actual vector database returns low-relevance noise that should have been filtered. The behavior depends on the database's similarity threshold enforcement, filtering logic, and index boundaries. Testing requires a real vector database instance with a known corpus and configured relevance thresholds.
behaviors.test_description: Load a real vector database with a known, representative corpus. Execute queries deliberately out-of-distribution (unrelated domains, nonsense, or highly dissimilar to corpus content). Verify the database returns either an empty result set or results below configured similarity threshold. Test edge cases: empty corpus, single-item corpus, corpus with only dissimilar items, and extremely common terms that might inflate similarity scores.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Mocked components (embedding service, vector DB, LLM) would have negligible latency and always pass, hiding real failures where combined latency exceeds 2 seconds. The behavior depends on real latency from all components: embedding model (API call or subprocess), vector database search (network I/O or local I/O), and LLM re-ranking (API call, typically 500-1000ms). Measuring end-to-end latency requires real external service calls and infrastructure. Workflow could also apply given the pipeline nature, but System best captures the requirement for real HTTP/API calls.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end requests through full pipeline with real service calls: actual embedding model (API or subprocess inference), real vector database queries, and actual LLM re-ranking calls. Measure per-component latency and total latency. Run 100+ representative requests with diverse query types (single-result, multi-result matches, large corpus searches). Measure p50, p95, and p99 latencies to ensure median stays below 2 seconds and tail latency is acceptable.
